verses.3: He won’t break a bruised reed.
verses.4: He will not fail nor be discouraged,
verses.6: “I, the LORD, have called you in righteousness.
verses.9: Behold, the former things have happened
verses.10: Sing to the LORD a new song,
verses.11: Let the wilderness and its cities raise their voices,
verses.12: Let them give glory to the LORD,
verses.13: The LORD will go out like a mighty man.
verses.14: “I have been silent a long time.
verses.15: I will destroy mountains and hills,
verses.16: I will bring the blind by a way that they don’t know.
verses.19: Who is blind, but my servant?
verses.20: You see many things, but don’t observe.
verses.21: It pleased the LORD, for his righteousness’ sake, to magnify the law
verses.22: But this is a robbed and plundered people.
verses.23: Who is there among you who will give ear to this?
verses.25: Therefore he poured the fierceness of his anger on him,
